zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Wilcock於1525年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾福洛可頓時，他的父親Anthony Wilcock25歲，母親Mrs. Anthony Wilcock20歲。他於1545年與Ann Watterhouse在英國越可爾米費爾德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1583年在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾卡爾頓享年58歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾卡爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Wilcock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: Wilcockson
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Nottinghamshire): patronymic meaning ‘son of   Wilcock  ’.

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Wilcott
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): habitational name from Willicote in Clifford Chambers (Gloucestershire). The placename derives from Old English wilig ‘willow’ + cot ‘cottage, hut, shelter’. In some cases perhaps an altered form of Wilcock .

variants.name: Wilcox
meanings: English (Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Flintshire): variant of Wilcock , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This form of the surname is based on the pronunciation.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Wilcock was born in 1525, in Flockton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Anthony Wilcock, was 25 and his mother, Mrs. Anthony Wilcock, was 20. He married Ann Watterhouse in 1545, in Mirfield, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1583, in Carleton, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Carleton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Ann Watterhouse en 1545, à Mirfield, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1583, à Carleton, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Carleton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Wilcock(이)가 1525년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 플록턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Anthony Wilcock(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Anthony Wilcock(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1545년 영국 요르크실르 미르필드에서 Ann Watterhouse(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1583년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 칼턴에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 칼턴에 묻혔다.
